section.p: A practical walk-through of every screen in the customer portal — what each button does, what you'll see, and what to expect.

div.span: Use the table of contents on the left to jump to any section. Every section is self-contained, so you can read just the part you need.

section.h2: What you can do with SUDUD
section.p: SUDUD offers two product lines you can mix and match depending on what you need:

div.p: You get a private Odoo instance on your own subdomain. You decide how many databases to put on it, install whichever apps you need, and have full admin access from inside the instance.
Ready-made business apps we deploy for you on shared infrastructure. Pick one, pay, and start using it in minutes — no setup on your end.

img.alt: The SUDUD home page showing the Services and Hosting offerings

figure.figcaption: The home page — entry points for both product lines.

section.p: Before you can purchase anything you need a free account. Sign-up takes about a minute.

ol.li: From the top right of any page, click Sign Up. (If you already have an account, click Sign In instead.)
Enter your name, work email and a password. The password must be at least 8 characters — the strength meter under the field tells you how strong it is.
Check the email we send you and click the confirmation link. Your account is active from that moment.

img.alt: Sign-up form with name, email and password fields

figure.figcaption: Sign-up form. The password strength meter updates as you type.

div.span: Use a real email — every invoice, snapshot notification and password reset goes there.

section.p: Want to try before you buy? You can spin up a 14-day trial instance without entering payment details.

ol.li: On the home page, look for the green Try hosting for X days free banner at the top.
Click Start free trial. You'll be taken to the plan-builder with trial mode pre-selected.
Choose your subdomain (e.g. my-company → my-company.sudud.com) and pick the resources you want. The trial uses a modest default — you can change it once you upgrade to a paid plan.
Click Start trial. Your instance starts deploying immediately — no credit card needed.

img.alt: Green free-trial banner at the top of the home page

figure.figcaption: The trial banner appears at the top of every public page during your eligibility window.

div.span: Trial instances don't include daily snapshots — that's a paid add-on. Make sure you upgrade before the trial ends if you want your data to keep running.

section.p: Whether you're a new customer or an existing one upgrading from a trial, the flow is the same.

ol.li: Click Hosting in the top navigation (or Services for a ready-made app).
Pick your subdomain. We'll check availability as you type — a green check means it's free, red means it's already taken.

Adjust the resource sliders:
Workers — how many concurrent users your instance can handle smoothly.
Storage — how much disk space your databases and attachments get.
Billing period — monthly or yearly. Yearly saves you about 20% off the monthly rate.

(Optional but recommended) Tick Add daily snapshots — nightly snapshots of every database plus your files and configuration, kept for up to 7 days. You can also add this later from your instance page.
Click Continue to payment. Enter your card details on the secure payment widget; we don't see the card number ourselves — it goes straight to the payment provider.
Once payment confirms, your instance starts provisioning. The page auto-refreshes when it's ready — usually 2-5 minutes for a fresh instance.

img.alt: Plan builder with sliders for workers and storage plus daily-backup checkbox

figure.figcaption: The plan builder. The order summary on the right updates live as you change the sliders.

section.p: After purchase you land on the instance detail page. This is your home base for everything related to one instance.
The page shows you at a glance:

ul.li: Status — Running, Stopped, Suspended, etc. The status pill at the top is colour-coded.
Quick action buttons for the most common operations: Open the instance, Manage databases, View snapshots, Stop / Start, Change plan, etc.
Recent invoices on the right column.

img.alt: Instance detail page showing status, quick actions, and recent invoices

figure.figcaption: The instance detail page. Action buttons appear/disappear based on the instance state.

div.span: If your instance is hosting and you haven't enabled daily snapshots yet, you'll see a blue prompt on this page asking you to turn them on — see Enabling snapshots below.

section.p: On a hosting instance you can host many databases under the same subdomain. Click Manage Databases from the instance page to open the database manager.

img.alt: Databases page listing each database with action buttons

figure.figcaption: Every database gets a row with five actions: Open, Backup Now, Repair Feature, Reset Password, Duplicate, Delete.

ol.li: At the top of the Databases page, click Create Database.

Fill in:
Name — lowercase letters, digits, single hyphens or underscores. The full name will be prefixed with your subdomain.
Admin login — your email by default. This is the user you'll sign in as.
Admin password — at least 6 characters.
Language — the interface language for that database.
Click Create. The page freezes briefly with a "Creating database…" overlay, then refreshes automatically when the database is ready (usually about a minute).

img.alt: Create database modal with name, admin login, password and language fields

figure.figcaption: Create-database modal. The "subdomain_" prefix is added automatically.

section.p: Useful when you want a sandbox copy of a production database to test something risky on.

ol.li:
Find the database row you want to copy, then click
Duplicate .
Type a new name (the original keeps its name; this one gets a fresh one).
Click Duplicate. The button spins until the copy is ready, then the page refreshes.

img.alt: Duplicate database modal asking for a new name

section.p: Forgot your admin password? You can reset it yourself — no support ticket needed.

ol.li:
Find the database row and click
Reset Password .
Inside the modal, a blue banner shows you which admin login this reset is for — make a note of it if you forgot the username too.
Type the new password twice (at least 6 characters) and click Reset password.
When the page reloads you'll see a green banner confirming the reset along with the login to use. Sign in to that database with the new password.

img.alt: Reset password modal showing the admin login and two password fields

section.p: If one of your databases starts showing an error page after a module change, or you can't reach the in-app Apps menu, the Repair Feature tool can fix it without losing data.

ol.li:
Click
Repair Feature
on the affected database row.

Type the name of the feature to re-install. Common picks:
base — rebuild the core (safe, usually quick)
web — rebuild the interface shell
all — repair every installed feature (slowest but the broadest fix)
Or a specific feature name like account or sale if you know which one started failing.
Click Run upgrade. A full-screen overlay appears while your instance is briefly paused, the feature is re-installed, and the instance comes back up. The page reloads automatically.
After the reload, you'll see a green banner with a collapsible Show full report — open it if you want the technical detail.

img.alt: Repair-feature modal with the feature name input field and common picks list

div.span: Your instance will be unavailable for 30 seconds to a few minutes while the repair runs. Don't close the tab.

section.p: Permanent and irreversible — make sure you have a backup if there's anything you want to keep.

ol.li: Click Delete on the database row.
To confirm, retype the full database name exactly. This is on purpose — to make sure you don't delete the wrong one by mistake.
Click Delete permanently. The button spins until the database (and any on-demand backups for it) is gone, then the page refreshes.

img.alt: Delete database confirmation modal asking to retype the database name

section.p: Need a one-off, downloadable copy of a single database — say, before doing a big import, or to migrate data elsewhere? Use Backup Now.

ol.li:
On the Databases page, click
Backup Now
on the database row.
The button spins while we prepare the backup. The page also shows a Backing up badge next to the database name. This takes anywhere from a few seconds to a few minutes depending on the database size.
When the page refreshes automatically, the database row gets a Backup ready badge and a Download Backup button. Click it to download the zip file.

img.alt: Database row with Backup Now button, in-progress badge, and Download Backup link

div.span: Only one on-demand backup per instance can be active at a time. While one is "Backup ready", the Backup Now button is disabled — clicking it again replaces the existing one with a fresh backup of the same database. The download link expires after 24 hours.

section.p: Daily snapshots capture every database on your instance plus your uploaded files, your custom modules and your instance settings — all in a single restorable snapshot. Up to 7 snapshots are kept (the oldest is dropped automatically).
Snapshots are an opt-in paid add-on. Billed monthly, independent of your main subscription cycle — even on a yearly plan you only pay one month at a time for the snapshot feature.

ol.li: On your instance page, look for the blue Protect this instance with daily snapshots prompt. Click Enable Daily Backups.
You land on the activation checkout page. The price is pre-rated for the remaining days of the current calendar month, so you only pay for what you actually use.
Enter your card details and submit. As soon as the payment confirms, snapshots are active and the next nightly cron (at 03:00 UTC) will create your first one.
From there on, your instance page shows a green Daily Backups Active badge.

img.alt: Enable Daily Backups prompt with the green call-to-action

div.span: Once enabled, snapshots can't be disabled from the portal — please contact support if you genuinely need to turn them off. We made this irreversible on purpose so nobody accidentally loses their nightly protection.

section.p: Restoring rolls your instance back in place to the exact state captured in the snapshot — same databases, same data, same files. It doesn't create a new snapshot, so your list stays exactly as you see it.

ol.li: From your instance page, click View Snapshots.
Pick the snapshot you want to roll back to and click Restore.
Confirm by retyping your subdomain — same idea as the delete-database guard: makes sure you don't restore by accident.
A full-screen overlay appears while we roll your instance back. This usually takes a few minutes. The page reloads automatically when your instance is back online.

img.alt: Snapshots page listing 7 dated entries with restore buttons

figure.figcaption: The snapshots page. Each row shows when the snapshot was taken and its size.

div.span: Anything that exists on your instance now but wasn't in the snapshot (e.g. a database you created after the snapshot was taken) is removed on restore. The snapshot's version of the world fully replaces the current one.

section.p: Need more workers or storage? Or downsizing? You can change plan at any time.

ol.li: On your instance page, click Change Plan.
Adjust the resource sliders to the new size. The order summary on the right shows you the price difference, prorated for the remaining days of your current billing cycle.
For upgrades (more resources): we issue a prorated upgrade invoice — pay it and the new plan applies immediately.
For downgrades (less resources): the change is scheduled to take effect at the start of your next billing cycle. Until then your instance keeps the current plan with no service interruption.

img.alt: Change-plan builder with current and new plan side by side

section.p: Decisions change — you can cancel an instance and reactivate it later.
Cancellation is initiated by our support team. Once it runs, here's what happens to your data:

ul.li: Your databases, files and custom modules are deleted.

The most recent snapshot is retained
— every other snapshot is dropped. The retained snapshot is the one you'll be able to restore from if you reactivate.

ol.li: Sign in. Cancelled instances show up on your instances list with a "Cancelled" badge.
Click Reactivate Instance on the cancelled instance and pick a new plan + billing period.
Pay the new subscription invoice. Your instance redeploys fresh.
Once it's running, click View Snapshots from the instance page. The retained snapshot is listed there — click Restore to bring your data back.

img.alt: Cancelled instance card with the Reactivate button

section.p: Every charge produces an invoice. You can view, pay and download all of them from your portal.

ul.li: Find Recent invoices on your instance detail page, or click Profile → Invoices in the top right to see all of them.
Unpaid invoices show a yellow "Complete payment" link.
Paid invoices have a download button — click it to grab the PDF.

img.alt: Invoice list showing paid and unpaid invoices with action buttons

section.h3: My instance is showing an error page — what do I do?
section.p: Use the Repair Feature tool on the affected database. Start with base; if that doesn't fix it, try all. If you're still stuck after that, contact support and we'll take a look.
Use Reset Password. The modal also tells you which admin login it's resetting, in case you forgot that too.
section.h3: I created a database with the wrong name.
section.p: Use Delete to remove it, then create a new one with the correct name. There's no rename action — Odoo database renaming has too many side effects.
section.h3: How much do daily snapshots cost?
section.p: The price is set by the platform operator and shown on the activation checkout page. Always billed monthly — even on a yearly plan.
Not directly. Daily snapshots are restore-only on-platform. For a portable, downloadable copy of one database, use Backup Now.
section.h3: Can I restore a snapshot taken on a different instance?
section.p: No — snapshots are tied to the instance that created them. To move data between instances, use Backup Now on the source, then upload the resulting zip on the destination using the in-Odoo database manager.
section.h3: My page didn't refresh on its own after an action.
section.p: Most actions auto-refresh within 5 seconds of completing. If the page is stuck on the in-progress overlay for more than a few minutes, refresh the browser manually — your action has likely already finished. If the in-progress banner is still there after that refresh, contact support.
section.h3: I need help that isn't covered here.
section.p: Reach us via the Contact page — we aim to respond within one business day.
